title: Method and system for abandoning a cased boreole
abstract: A milling and scraping tool for preparing a borehole to be plugged includes a tool body including a first end, a second end, and an intermediate portion extending therebetween and a milling system arranged between the first end and the second end. The milling system includes one or more selectively deployable milling blades. The one or more selectively deployable milling blades is shiftable between a retracted position and a deployed position. A scraping system IS arranged on the tool body. The scraping system includes a plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades selectively deployable from a retracted position to a deployed position.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A milling and scraping tool (12) for preparing a borehole (20) to be plugged, the milling and scraping tool (12) comprising: a tool body (31) including a first end (32), a second end (33), and an intermediate portion (34) extending therebetween; a milling system (50) arranged between the first end (32) and the second end (33), the milling system (50) including one or more selectively deployable milling blades (54), the one or more selectively deployable milling blades (54) being shiftable between a retracted position and a deployed position; and a scraping system (90) arranged on the tool body (31), the scraping system (90) including a plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) selectively deployable from a retracted position to a deployed position. 2. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 1, further comprising: a locking mechanism (69) operatively connected to the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94), the locking mechanism (69) selectively locking the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) in each of the retracted position and the deployed position. 3. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 2, further comprising: another locking mechanism (69) operatively associated with the one or more selectively deployable milling blades (94). 4. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 1, further comprising: at least one telemetry and power module (104) operable to communicate with a surface system. 5. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 4, wherein the at least one telemetry and power module (104) is operable to communicate with the surface system (4) through one of a wired connection and a wireless connection. 6. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 4, wherein the at least one telemetry and power module (104) includes a first telemetry and power module (104) operatively associated with the milling system (50) and a second telemetry and power module (104) operatively associated with the scraping system (90). 7. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 1, further comprising: an activation mechanism (56) operatively associated with at least one of the milling system (50) and the scraping system (90), the activation mechanism (56) being operable to selectively shift at least one of the one or more selectively deployable milling blades (54) and 

 the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) between the retracted position and the deployed position. 8. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 7, further comprising a sensor (120) operatively associated with at least one of the activation mechanism (56) and at least one of the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94), the sensor (120) being operable to determine a position of the selectively deployable plurality of scraping blades (94). 9. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 1, further comprising: a hydraulic pump and piston module (107) operatively associated with the scraping system (96), the hydraulic pump and piston module (107) being operable to deliver a stream of fluid from the milling and scraping tool (12) during a scraping operation. 10. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 9, wherein the hydraulic pump and piston module (107) is operable to selectively shift the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) between the deployed position and retracted position. 11. The milling and scraping tool (12) according to claim 9, further comprising: a hydraulic pump module (63) operatively associated with the milling system (50), the hydraulic pump module (63) being operable to deliver a stream of fluid from the milling and scraping tool (12) during a milling operation. 12. A method of preparing a borehole (20) for abandonment comprising: running a milling and scraping tool (12) into a borehole (20) having an inner casing (28) and an outer casing (26) separated by cement; cutting the inner casing (28) with a plurality of selectively deployable milling blades (54) of a milling system portion (50) of the milling and scraping tool (12); and scraping away the cement up to the outer casing (26) with a plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) of a scraping system portion (90) of the milling and scraping tool (12). 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: circulating fluid over the plurality of selectively deployable milling blades (54) while cutting the inner casing (28). 14. The method of claim 12, further comprising: circulating fluid over the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) while scraping away the cement. 15. The method of claim 12, further comprising: signaling to the scraping system portion (90) to shift the plurality of selectively deployable scraping blades (94) to a deployed position.
